decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to utilize the powerful combo of Parallax Wave and Opalescence to control the board and eventually win the game. By casting Replenish, players can bring back key enchantments from the graveyard, creating a strong board presence. Cards like Decree of Silence and Tsabo's Web help to disrupt opponents' strategies, while Frantic Search and Attunement provide card advantage and help set up the combo. With the right sequencing and card choices, this deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through its combo strategy.

This goblin deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. By using cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Warchief to quickly put creatures on the battlefield, along with support cards like Rishadan Port and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base, this deck aims to swarm the board with powerful goblin creatures like Goblin Piledriver and Siege-Gang Commander. With a mix of burn spells like Mogg Fanatic and Gempalm Incinerator, as well as tutor effects from cards like Goblin Matron, this deck aims to quickly deal damage and overrun the opponent before they can stabilize.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells, hand disruption, and removal spells to protect Psychatog, which is the main win condition. The deck focuses on card advantage with spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Fact or Fiction, while also having additional tools like Wonder and Tsabo's Web to deal with specific threats. The land base is optimized for consistency and mana fixing, allowing the deck to smoothly execute its game plan of out-valuing and outlasting the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the combination of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to quickly assemble a powerful creature while denying the opponent's resources. By using cards like Vision Charm, Counterspell, and Foil to protect the combo, as well as card draw spells like Gush and Accumulated Knowledge to find the pieces needed, this deck aims to win the game through an explosive and unexpected sequence of plays. Additionally, cards like Daze and Chain of Vapor provide further disruption and flexibility in dealing with opposing threats.

This deck focuses on quickly dropping mana-producing elves to ramp up to powerful creatures like Masticore and Deranged Hermit. The use of Quirion Ranger and Wirewood Symbiote allows for extra mana generation and card advantage. Survival of the Fittest is key to finding the right creatures for each situation, while Gaea's Cradle provides a massive mana boost. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and efficient aggro strategy, fueled by the synergy of the elves in the deck.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game while also growing your own creatures to overwhelm your opponent. This is achieved by using counterspells and removal spells to control the board, while also using cards like Accumulated Knowledge and Gush to draw cards and power up creatures like Psychatog and Quirion Dryad. By using a combination of control and proactive growth strategies, this deck aims to dominate the game and secure victory in a Premodern 1 vs 1 format.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. By utilizing powerful control cards such as Mana Leak and Counterspell, along with efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of God, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while slowly gaining an advantage. Standstill is a key card in this deck, allowing the player to draw extra cards while potentially locking down the game. The deck also includes utility lands like Dust Bowl to further control the battlefield.

The deck focuses on using Stifle to disrupt opponents while utilizing cards like Phyrexian Dreadnought and Flash of Insight to create a powerful combo. The goal is to quickly assemble the Dreadnought combo and protect it with counterspells and other disruptive spells. The deck also includes card draw and selection spells like Opt and Portent to ensure consistency in finding important pieces of the combo. The key strategy is to control the game early on and then quickly close out the game with the combo.

The main focus of this Goblins Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of aggressive and synergistic Goblin creatures. By utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Warchief to quickly get creatures onto the battlefield, and cards like Goblin Piledriver and Siege-Gang Commander to deal massive amounts of damage, the deck aims to maintain pressure and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Support cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Naturalize help to deal with threats and maintain control on the board, while lands like Rishadan Port and Wasteland provide additional disruption to the opponent's resources.

This deck focuses on using a variety of removal spells and hand disruption to control the game, while also utilizing powerful creatures like Withered Wretch and Ravenous Baloth to apply pressure. The inclusion of cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress allows for strategic disruption of the opponent's game plan, while cards like Pernicious Deed provide board control. The Rock archetype emphasizes a mix of resource denial and proactive threats to outlast and outmaneuver opponents in a 1v1 match-up.

The deck focuses on quickly discarding high-cost creatures into the graveyard, then using spells like Reanimate, Exhume, and Animate Dead to bring them back onto the battlefield at a reduced cost. This allows the player to overwhelm their opponent with powerful creatures such as Akroma, Angel of Wrath and Verdant Force. Additionally, cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy help disrupt the opponent's strategy while Unmask provides further discard options. The goal is to establish a strong board presence early on and quickly close out the game before the opponent can respond effectively.

This premodern deck focuses on using a combination of cards to quickly and efficiently bring out Phyrexian Dreadnought while preventing your opponent from countering or stopping the combo. With a mix of card draw, counterspells, and stalling tactics like Stifle and Foil, the deck aims to establish board control and win through the Dreadnought's powerful abilities. By utilizing cards like Gush and Opt to dig through the deck and find the necessary pieces, this deck aims to quickly assemble its combo and overpower the opponent before they have a chance to react effectively.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo with Stifle, allowing you to cheat out a powerful creature for a minimal cost. The deck includes a variety of card draw and control spells to help dig for combo pieces and protect your board state. With the ability to counter spells and disrupt your opponent's game plan, this deck aims to swiftly close out games with a huge, hard-hitting threat.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana using Mox Diamond and Weathered Wayfarer to get ahead of the opponent. It aims to disrupt the opponent's mana base with cards like Rishadan Port and Wasteland while also playing aggressive threats like Terravore and Exalted Angel. The deck then uses Armageddon to reset the board and take advantage of its own land destruction, pulling ahead with its resilient creatures and threat of recurring Armageddon effects. The strategy is to create a resource imbalance in the player's favor and overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat while disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells and card draw. The deck aims to control the game tempo and protect its key pieces while digging for the combo using cantrips and card draw spells. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's plays, the deck can quickly overwhelm the opponent with a massive creature and secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with aggressive Goblin creatures, utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey to put threats into play for free, while also using cards like Goblin Warchief to reduce costs and accelerate the board presence. The deck includes removal spells like Gempalm Incinerator and Mogg Fanatic to deal with opposing threats, as well as powerful finishers like Goblin Piledriver and Siege-Gang Commander to close out the game. The deck aims to maintain pressure and control the board to secure victory before the opponent can stabilize.
